title: No committed secrets
description: Secrets, tokens, private keys, or production credentials must never appear in code, logs, fixtures, screenshots, or commit history.
instruction: Do not add secrets, tokens, private keys, or credentials to tracked files. Load them from environment variables or a secret manager.

examples.text: good: satisfy No committed secrets; bad: bypass it

limitations: Automated checks cover only registered patterns; review other implementations.

title: No swallowed errors
description: Errors must not be silently discarded. An intentionally ignored error must be documented with the reason, and broad exception handling must add context and re-raise or convert.
instruction: Do not catch and suppress errors. Convert unknown external errors into typed domain errors, add context before propagating, and never leave a bare or empty except.

examples.text: good: satisfy No swallowed errors; bad: bypass it

limitations: Automated checks cover only registered patterns; review other implementations.
Language-specific behavior may differ outside registered checks.

title: No broad exception handling
description: Exception handlers must catch only the specific failures an operation can raise.
instruction: Catch specific exception types. Do not use bare except or catch Exception without converting, contextualizing, or re-raising it.

examples.text: good: satisfy No broad exception handling; bad: bypass it

limitations: Automated checks cover only registered patterns; review other implementations.
Language-specific behavior may differ outside registered checks.

title: External calls require timeouts
description: Network, database, subprocess, lock, and external API operations must have explicit bounded timeouts.
instruction: Add an explicit timeout and ensure cancellation or cleanup is handled.

examples.text: good: satisfy External calls require timeouts; bad: bypass it

limitations: Automated checks cover only registered patterns; review other implementations.
Language-specific behavior may differ outside registered checks.

title: Validate public input
description: Public request input must be validated before use.
instruction: Validate and normalize public input with an explicit schema before use.

examples.text: good: satisfy Validate public input; bad: bypass it

limitations: Automated checks cover only registered patterns; review other implementations.
Language-specific behavior may differ outside registered checks.

title: Parameterize SQL
description: SQL statements must not interpolate untrusted values.
instruction: Use driver parameters for every dynamic SQL value.

examples.text: good: satisfy Parameterize SQL; bad: bypass it

limitations: Automated checks cover only registered patterns; review other implementations.
Language-specific behavior may differ outside registered checks.

title: Bound retries and loops
description: Retry and worker loops must have explicit termination bounds.
instruction: Add an explicit attempt, time, or cancellation bound.

examples.text: good: satisfy Bound retries and loops; bad: bypass it

limitations: Automated checks cover only registered patterns; review other implementations.
Language-specific behavior may differ outside registered checks.

title: Safeguard destructive operations
description: Recursive destructive operations require explicit safeguards.
instruction: Require explicit confirmation and validate the target before recursive deletion.

examples.text: good: satisfy Safeguard destructive operations; bad: bypass it

limitations: Automated checks cover only registered patterns; review other implementations.
Language-specific behavior may differ outside registered checks.

title: Regression tests required
description: Observable bug fixes require regression coverage.
instruction: Add a deterministic regression test for observable bug fixes.

examples.text: good: satisfy Regression tests required; bad: bypass it

limitations: Automated checks cover only registered patterns; review other implementations.
Language-specific behavior may differ outside registered checks.

title: New behavior tests required
description: Source behavior changes require corresponding tests.
instruction: Add deterministic tests for new or changed source behavior.

examples.text: good: satisfy New behavior tests required; bad: bypass it

limitations: Automated checks cover only registered patterns; review other implementations.
Language-specific behavior may differ outside registered checks.

title: Preserve unrelated user changes
description: Do not modify files outside the current task scope.
instruction: Preserve unrelated work and restrict edits to files recorded for this task.

examples.text: good: satisfy Preserve unrelated user changes; bad: bypass it

limitations: Automated checks cover only registered patterns; review other implementations.

title: Review new dependencies
description: Dependency changes require focused risk review.
instruction: Review necessity, license, maintenance, transitive size, and security posture.

examples.text: good: satisfy Review new dependencies; bad: bypass it

limitations: Automated checks cover only registered patterns; review other implementations.

title: Review auth and security changes
description: Authentication and security-sensitive changes require focused review.
instruction: Perform a focused authentication, authorization, and sensitive-data review.

examples.text: good: satisfy Review auth and security changes; bad: bypass it

limitations: Automated checks cover only registered patterns; review other implementations.

title: Required repository commands pass
description: Repository-configured validation commands must pass before Stop completes.
instruction: Run every configured repository validation command and fix failures.

examples.text: good: satisfy Required repository commands pass; bad: bypass it

limitations: Automated checks cover only registered patterns; review other implementations.

title: Verification claims require evidence
description: Assistant verification claims must match current successful command evidence.
instruction: Do not claim a command or tests passed unless current Stop evidence proves exit status 0.

examples.text: good: satisfy Verification claims require evidence; bad: bypass it

limitations: Automated checks cover only registered patterns; review other implementations.

title: Forbid unreviewed Rust unsafe
description: Rust unsafe requires approved invariants and architecture review.

examples.text: good: use the supported pattern; bad: bypass it

limitations: The native check is lexical and requires review for complex syntax.
instruction: Rust unsafe requires approved invariants and architecture review.

title: Avoid Rust unwrap and expect
description: Production Rust paths should use typed error handling.

examples.text: good: use the supported pattern; bad: bypass it

limitations: The native check is lexical and requires review for complex syntax.
instruction: Production Rust paths should use typed error handling.

title: Avoid TypeScript any
description: TypeScript boundaries should use unknown or a precise type.

examples.text: good: use the supported pattern; bad: bypass it

limitations: The native check is lexical and requires review for complex syntax.
instruction: TypeScript boundaries should use unknown or a precise type.

title: Do not mutate React state
description: React state must be updated through setters, never mutated in place.

examples.text: good: use the supported pattern; bad: bypass it

limitations: The native check is lexical and requires review for complex syntax.
instruction: React state must be updated through setters, never mutated in place.

title: Use stable React keys
description: React list keys should use stable domain IDs rather than indexes.

examples.text: good: use the supported pattern; bad: bypass it

limitations: The native check is lexical and requires review for complex syntax.
instruction: React list keys should use stable domain IDs rather than indexes.

title: Narrow external JSON
description: External JSON must be parsed as unknown and narrowed before use.

examples.text: good: validate at the boundary; bad: trust raw JSON

limitations: The lexical check identifies common boundary calls and requires review for wrappers and schema libraries.
instruction: External JSON must be parsed as unknown and narrowed before use.

title: Validate API responses
description: API responses require explicit runtime validation before use.

examples.text: good: validate at the boundary; bad: trust raw JSON

limitations: The lexical check identifies common boundary calls and requires review for wrappers and schema libraries.
instruction: API responses require explicit runtime validation before use.

title: Bound Rust spawned tasks
description: Spawned Rust tasks require cancellation and error reporting.

examples.text: good: propagate cancellation and errors; bad: detach work

limitations: The lexical check identifies common spawn/global syntax and requires review for wrappers and macros.
instruction: Spawned Rust tasks require cancellation and error reporting.

title: Avoid mutable Rust globals
description: Mutable Rust globals require explicit architecture review.

examples.text: good: propagate cancellation and errors; bad: detach work

limitations: The lexical check identifies common spawn/global syntax and requires review for wrappers and macros.
instruction: Mutable Rust globals require explicit architecture review.

title: Review React effect cleanup
description: React effects must clean up subscriptions, timers, observers, sockets, and async work.

examples.text: good: make the state explicit; bad: rely on implicit null behavior

limitations: This is review guidance; semantic UI quality is not claimed as static proof.
instruction: React effects must clean up subscriptions, timers, observers, sockets, and async work.

title: Review React error and loading states
description: Interactive React surfaces should expose explicit loading and failure states.

examples.text: good: make the state explicit; bad: rely on implicit null behavior

limitations: This is review guidance; semantic UI quality is not claimed as static proof.
instruction: Interactive React surfaces should expose explicit loading and failure states.

title: Review React accessibility semantics
description: React components should use semantic labels, keyboard paths, focus management, and accessible states.

examples.text: good: make the state explicit; bad: rely on implicit null behavior

limitations: This is review guidance; semantic UI quality is not claimed as static proof.
instruction: React components should use semantic labels, keyboard paths, focus management, and accessible states.

title: Review Rust external async timeouts
description: External Rust async work should use explicit timeout and cancellation boundaries.

examples.text: good: make boundaries typed and cancellable; bad: pass loose strings or detached work

limitations: This is review guidance; semantic async and domain modeling proof is not claimed.
instruction: External Rust async work should use explicit timeout and cancellation boundaries.

title: Review Rust IDs and units
description: Rust IDs, units, and validated strings should use domain newtypes at boundaries.

examples.text: good: make boundaries typed and cancellable; bad: pass loose strings or detached work

limitations: This is review guidance; semantic async and domain modeling proof is not claimed.
instruction: Rust IDs, units, and validated strings should use domain newtypes at boundaries.

title: Check Go ignored errors
description: Go errors should be checked or deliberately documented, not discarded.

examples.text: good: handle and wrap causes; bad: discard or detach errors

limitations: The lexical check identifies common patterns and requires review for interfaces, generated code, and wrappers.
instruction: Go errors should be checked or deliberately documented, not discarded.

title: Review Go goroutine cancellation
description: Go goroutines require cancellation and error reporting.

examples.text: good: handle and wrap causes; bad: discard or detach errors

limitations: The lexical check identifies common patterns and requires review for interfaces, generated code, and wrappers.
instruction: Go goroutines require cancellation and error reporting.

title: Review Go mutable globals
description: Go package-level mutable state requires explicit review.

examples.text: good: handle and wrap causes; bad: discard or detach errors

limitations: The lexical check identifies common patterns and requires review for interfaces, generated code, and wrappers.
instruction: Go package-level mutable state requires explicit review.

title: Review Go error wrapping
description: Go wrapped errors should preserve causes with %w where appropriate.

examples.text: good: handle and wrap causes; bad: discard or detach errors

limitations: The lexical check identifies common patterns and requires review for interfaces, generated code, and wrappers.
instruction: Go wrapped errors should preserve causes with %w where appropriate.

title: Review Go context placement
description: Request-scoped Go functions should accept context.Context as the first parameter.

examples.text: good: func Run(ctx context.Context, ...); bad: hide context in globals

limitations: Context placement is review guidance until grammar-backed analysis is available.
instruction: Review request-scoped Go functions for context.Context as the first parameter.

title: Review oversized functions
description: Functions over 50 lines require extraction or a documented parser/table/state-machine exemption.

examples.text: good: split cohesive responsibilities; bad: grow one multi-concern function

limitations: Lexical spans do not prove semantic cohesion; untouched legacy debt is not baselined yet.
instruction: Keep functions near 20–30 lines and split before 50 unless a documented exemption applies.

title: Review oversized files
description: Files over 300 lines require review and should be split before 500 lines.

examples.text: good: keep one abstraction level; bad: combine unrelated branches

limitations: Lexical metrics are review signals; generated code and legacy baselines need repository policy.
instruction: Files over 300 lines require review and should be split before 500 lines.

title: Review function complexity
description: Functions should keep parameters, nesting, and cyclomatic complexity bounded.

examples.text: good: keep one abstraction level; bad: combine unrelated branches

limitations: Lexical metrics are review signals; generated code and legacy baselines need repository policy.
instruction: Functions should keep parameters, nesting, and cyclomatic complexity bounded.

title: Review shell safety
description: Quote variables, use safe parsing, and check command availability in shell glue.

examples.text: good: validate and bound the change; bad: apply unreviewed mutable configuration

limitations: This is review guidance; semantic shell/IaC/config safety is not claimed as static proof.
instruction: Quote variables, use safe parsing, and check command availability in shell glue.

title: Review shell idempotency
description: Shell automation should be idempotent and use bounded failure handling.

examples.text: good: validate and bound the change; bad: apply unreviewed mutable configuration

limitations: This is review guidance; semantic shell/IaC/config safety is not claimed as static proof.
instruction: Shell automation should be idempotent and use bounded failure handling.

title: Review infrastructure validation
description: Infrastructure changes require format, validation, least privilege, and rollback evidence.

examples.text: good: validate and bound the change; bad: apply unreviewed mutable configuration

limitations: This is review guidance; semantic shell/IaC/config safety is not claimed as static proof.
instruction: Infrastructure changes require format, validation, least privilege, and rollback evidence.

title: Review configuration schemas
description: YAML, JSON, and shared configuration should use schemas and avoid duplicate keys or secret outputs.

examples.text: good: validate and bound the change; bad: apply unreviewed mutable configuration

limitations: This is review guidance; semantic shell/IaC/config safety is not claimed as static proof.
instruction: YAML, JSON, and shared configuration should use schemas and avoid duplicate keys or secret outputs.

title: Review AI-assisted coding discipline
description: AI-assisted changes must stay within task scope, preserve unrelated work, avoid invented verification or unapproved deletion, and report residual/unrun risk.

examples.text: good: show touched files and executed checks; bad: invent a passing test or delete unapproved work

limitations: Evidence and diff checks cannot infer user intent or prove every generated name is non-generic.
instruction: Keep changes task-scoped, preserve unrelated work, never invent verification or delete unapproved files, and report unrun checks and residual risk.

title: Review commit and PR evidence
description: Completion summaries should state the problem, implementation, tests, security/performance, migration/rollback, screenshots, and residual risks without inventing evidence.

examples.text: good: list tests and residual risk; bad: claim green checks that did not run

limitations: The runtime does not push or message external systems; this is completion/PR guidance.
instruction: Summarize problem, implementation, tests, security, performance, migration, rollback, screenshots, and residual risks using only executed evidence.

title: Review refactor discipline
description: Refactors should separate mechanical and behavioral changes, retain tests, remove obsolete duplicates, and preserve public behavior unless evidence proves the change.

examples.text: good: split mechanical and behavioral commits; bad: replace code and leave duplicate paths

limitations: Architectural duplication and public-behavior preservation require review and tests.
instruction: Keep mechanical and behavioral changes separable; add behavior tests, remove obsolete duplicates, and preserve public behavior with evidence.

title: Review documentation and rollout changes
description: Setup/config/operations changes need README or runbook updates, public API docs, ADRs for irreversible decisions, and rollback/migration notes for risky changes.

examples.text: good: document config and rollback; bad: change deployment behavior with no operator notes

limitations: Documentation completeness is contextual review; UI screenshots are requested only when configured.
instruction: Update README/setup, public API docs, ADRs, runbooks, rollback/migration notes, and configured UI evidence when behavior or operations change.

title: Review dependency changes
description: Dependency changes need direct/transitive delta, license, source, pinning, maintenance/security evidence, runtime impact, and adapter review for volatile APIs.

examples.text: good: pin and record license/source impact; bad: add an unreviewed volatile dependency

limitations: Offline environments and package ecosystems vary; missing evidence remains unverified.
instruction: Review direct/transitive delta, license, source, pinning, maintenance/security evidence, runtime impact, and adapter seams for volatile dependencies.

title: Review performance-sensitive changes
description: Hot paths and performance work require a baseline and remeasurement, with review for N+1 queries, unpaginated lists, unbounded caches/queues, synchronous slow work, and repeated heavy renders.

examples.text: good: measure before and after a bounded query; bad: optimize a hot path without a baseline

limitations: Performance semantics require measured evidence; static guidance cannot prove latency or throughput.
instruction: For performance work, capture a baseline and remeasurement; review N+1/unpaginated work, unbounded caches/queues, synchronous slow calls, and repeated heavy renders.

title: Review endpoint security controls
description: Public and expensive routes need input validation, server-side authorization, rate limiting, secure cookies/CORS/CSRF, and non-debug defaults.

examples.text: good: validate, authorize, rate-limit; bad: trust raw public input and ship debug mode

limitations: Framework-specific auth, rate-limit, cookie, CORS, and CSRF semantics remain review unless a registered checker proves them.
instruction: For each public or expensive route, document runtime validation, server-side authentication/authorization, rate limiting, secure cookies/CORS/CSRF, and non-debug defaults; report unknown semantics as review.

title: Enforce public endpoint controls
description: Detected public endpoints require runtime input validation, server-side authentication and authorization, rate limiting, secure cookies/CORS/CSRF, and non-debug defaults.

examples.text: good: validate and authorize at the route boundary; bad: trust raw public input

limitations: Lexical signals cannot prove runtime authorization, rate-limit policy, cookie flags, CORS, CSRF, or debug configuration; complete signals remain unverified.
instruction: For each public or expensive route, prove boundary validation, server-side authorization, rate limiting, secure cookie/CORS/CSRF settings, and non-debug defaults with runtime evidence; static signals never claim semantic proof.

title: Review public endpoint controls
description: Detected public endpoints require boundary validation, server-side authentication/authorization, rate limits, and secure cookie/CORS/CSRF/debug defaults.

examples.text: good: validate and authorize at the route boundary; bad: expose raw request data with debug defaults

limitations: Signals cover common FastAPI, Express, and Next-style routes; unknown frameworks remain unverified rather than silently passing.
instruction: For public endpoints, require boundary validation, server-side auth/authorization, rate limits for expensive/public routes, secure cookies/CORS/CSRF, and non-debug defaults.

title: Review safe construction boundaries
description: Avoid concatenating untrusted values into shell, HTML, URL, JSON, regex, or SQL strings; use contextual builders and escaping.

examples.text: good: parameterized query/argv builder; bad: 'SELECT ' + user_input

limitations: Lexical checks cover common concatenation shapes; framework-specific builders and semantic escaping remain review.
instruction: Use parameterized SQL, argv/builders, contextual HTML/URL escaping, and typed serializers; do not concatenate untrusted values into boundary strings.

title: Require temporary-code justification
description: Temporary, TODO, disabled, or suppressed code needs a reason, owner, ISO expiry, and deletion condition.

examples.text: good: TODO reason=legacy owner=team expires=2099-01-01 delete=remove-after-migration; bad: TODO fix later

limitations: Existing debt is surfaced when touched; semantic completeness of a reason or deletion condition remains review.
instruction: Temporary or disabled code must carry reason=, owner=, expires=YYYY-MM-DD, and delete=... metadata; expired markers fail the gate.

title: Review SQL migrations and queries
description: SQL changes need parameterized input, explicit columns, transaction and constraint review, reversible migrations, rollback evidence, and hot-query plan review.

examples.text: good: explicit columns inside a transaction; bad: SELECT * and an irreversible migration with no rollback

limitations: Migration framework and query-plan semantics depend on configured SQL tooling and database evidence.
instruction: Use parameterized inputs and explicit columns; review transactions, constraints, reversibility, rollback, and hot-query plans with real database evidence.

title: Review C and C++ safety
description: C and C++ changes require warnings, ownership/bounds review, overflow awareness, sanitizers, and fuzz coverage where risk warrants.

examples.text: good: enable warnings and sanitizers; bad: unchecked pointer arithmetic without a regression or fuzz case

limitations: Compiler, sanitizer, and fuzz semantics depend on configured CMake/Meson/Make tooling.
instruction: Review warnings-as-errors, ownership and bounds, overflow, sanitizer, and fuzzing coverage; run configured CMake/Meson/Make gates without inventing tools.

title: Review C# boundaries
description: C# changes should preserve nullable contracts, async cancellation, immutable values, and dependency-injection lifetime safety.

examples.text: good: pass CancellationToken and bound service lifetimes; bad: fire-and-forget async work with nullable state

limitations: Roslyn analyzers, nullable settings, and DI lifetime semantics depend on configured dotnet projects.
instruction: Review nullable contracts, cancellation tokens, immutable value types, async lifetime safety, and DI scopes; run configured dotnet format/build/test gates.

title: Review Java and Kotlin boundaries
description: JVM changes should use nullability, immutable domain values, controller boundaries, structured concurrency, and typed errors.

examples.text: good: typed domain error at the boundary; bad: nullable controller state leaks into infrastructure

limitations: Formatter, analyzer, nullability, and concurrency semantics depend on configured Gradle/Maven tools.
instruction: Review nullability, immutable domain modeling, controller/infrastructure boundaries, structured concurrency, cancellation, and typed error conversion; run the workspace's configured Gradle/Maven gates.

title: Review UI accessibility signals
description: Semantic HTML and UI components need image alternatives and accessible labels or roles.

examples.text: good: ; bad:

limitations: Lexical checks cover common missing-alt/label patterns; keyboard, contrast, and visual semantics remain review.
instruction: Use semantic elements and provide accessible labels, roles, focus states, and image alternatives; verify rendered behavior.

title: Review responsive UI signals
description: UI changes should avoid brittle fixed styling and be checked at small, medium, and large viewports.

examples.text: good: use responsive tokens; bad: fixed !important dimensions everywhere

limitations: Static signals cannot prove layout stability or visual quality; screenshot evidence remains optional review.
instruction: Prefer design tokens and responsive layout; verify accessibility and visual behavior at configured small/medium/large viewports.

title: Review test names
description: Test names should describe the observable behavior under test instead of generic placeholders.

examples.text: good: rejects_expired_token; bad: test

limitations: Only generic parser symbols are flagged; framework-specific naming conventions remain review.
instruction: Name tests after the observable behavior and expected outcome they verify.

title: Review test determinism
description: Unit tests should avoid real sleeps, unseeded randomness, live network calls, shared mutable fixtures, and paid external dependencies unless explicitly marked integration/e2e.

examples.text: good: inject a clock and seed randomness; bad: sleep and call a live API in a unit test

limitations: Lexical signals cannot prove order dependence or paid dependencies; explicit integration/e2e paths are allowed.
instruction: Keep unit tests deterministic: inject clocks, seed randomness, avoid real sleeps/live network/paid dependencies, and isolate mutable fixtures; mark integration/e2e tests explicitly.

title: Review behavior-test assertions
description: Tests should assert observable outcomes rather than only running code or asserting an unconditional truth.

examples.text: good: assert_eq!(result, expected); bad: assert!(true)

limitations: Only high-confidence trivial assertions are flagged; semantic assertion strength remains review.
instruction: Assert observable outcomes and meaningful error cases; do not use smoke-only or unconditional truth assertions as proof.

title: Select appropriate test types
description: Choose test forms that match the changed behavior: table-driven and property tests for parsers/validators/classifiers, golden tests for stable generated output, contract tests at service/schema boundaries, and integration tests for public APIs.

examples.text: good: use a golden test for stable JSON output; bad: assert only that a function did not throw

limitations: Signals request review; they do not prove assertion strength or coverage percentage.
instruction: Match test style to the change: table-driven/property/fuzz tests for parsers and validators; golden tests for stable output; contract tests at service/schema boundaries; integration tests for public APIs.

title: Follow the debugging protocol
description: Bug fixes should record reproduction inputs, environment and version, one hypothesis, a root-cause repair, a regression test, and removal or conversion of temporary diagnostics.

examples.text: good: reproduce, state one hypothesis, repair the cause, add a regression test; bad: patch symptoms and leave debug prints

limitations: The packet can request protocol evidence but cannot prove semantic root cause or reproduction quality.
instruction: For bug fixes, state reproduction inputs/environment/version and one hypothesis; repair the root cause, add a regression test, and remove or convert temporary diagnostics.

title: Review sensitive logging
description: Logs must not expose passwords, tokens, cookies, auth headers, raw payloads, or PII.

examples.text: good: redact auth headers before logging; bad: logger.info('token=%s', token)

limitations: Lexical signals are review findings; wrapper output is sanitized and no secret value is echoed.
instruction: Redact or remove credentials, auth headers, cookies, PII, and raw payloads from logs; never echo secret values in findings.

title: Review structured service observability
description: Long-running services and workers should expose structured logs, correlation IDs, health checks, and metrics for latency, errors, queues, retries, and drops.

examples.text: good: correlate request logs and measure queue latency; bad: emit unstructured messages with no health signal

limitations: Static analysis does not prove semantic observability coverage; this rule only requests focused review.
instruction: Review structured log keys, correlation IDs, health checks, and metrics for latency, errors, queue depth, retries, and drops; do not claim static proof.

title: Review boundary error handling
description: External errors must be contextualized, converted, rethrown, or deliberately handled; empty exception paths are not acceptable.

examples.text: good: except ExternalError: raise DomainError('context'); bad: except Exception: pass

limitations: Lexical review covers clear empty Python and JavaScript-family handlers; typed conversion, cleanup, retries, and log duplication remain language-specific review.
instruction: At boundaries, add context and convert or rethrow unknown errors; do not leave empty exception handlers.

title: Apply contextual maintainability design guidance
description: When a task touches domain models, boundaries, or architecture, prefer illegal-state prevention, validated-domain conversion, pure cores with side-effect edges, explicit data flow, dependency injection for external effects, and simple designs before distributed abstractions.

examples.text: good: parse raw input into a validated domain type before a pure core; bad: pass unvalidated maps through side effects

limitations: This is contextual review guidance; semantic architecture and illegal-state proofs are not automated.
instruction: Model illegal states out of existence; convert raw input to validated domain values; keep pure core logic separate from side effects; make data flow explicit; inject time, randomness, network, and filesystem dependencies; avoid premature distributed or abstract architecture.

title: Review identifier naming
description: Use specific, verb-first names and avoid placeholder identifiers in production code.

examples.text: good: validate_request; bad: foo

limitations: Only clearly placeholder function names are flagged; naming quality and protocol fields remain review.
instruction: Use domain-specific, verb-first names; avoid placeholder identifiers such as foo, bar, tmp, or thing.

title: Review module boundaries
description: Modules should not form deterministic dependency cycles; domain code should remain separated from infrastructure concerns.

examples.text: good: depend through an adapter; bad: module A imports B while B imports A

limitations: Only relative source imports visible in the bounded touched-file graph are analyzed; architectural layering remains review guidance.
instruction: Avoid dependency cycles and keep domain code behind explicit adapter seams.

title: Review boundary error contracts
description: Boundary failures should expose an action, entity, specific reason, and retryability instead of opaque messages.

examples.text: good: config load failed: entity=config reason=missing retryable=false; bad: failed

limitations: The lexical check reviews newly added failure strings; it cannot prove every runtime boundary or typed error conversion.
instruction: At system boundaries, include action, entity, specific reason, and retryable=true|false in failure output.

title: Review anti-slop checklist signals
description: Remove debug output, scaffolding, broad suppressions, and temporary code from diffs.

examples.text: good: ship focused code; bad: leave debug output or temporary suppressions

limitations: The diff check covers high-confidence lexical signals only; architectural slop remains review.
instruction: Review the diff for debug prints, scaffolding, broad suppressions, and temporary code.
